title: Anxious attachment activation
summary: Fear of disconnection, reassurance-seeking, or intense preoccupation with relational security.
signals: i need to know they still care
i keep checking for a reply
i can't stop thinking about what they meant
interpretation: Possible attachment activation organized around uncertainty, closeness threat, and reassurance-seeking.
responseGuidance: Validate attachment alarm without escalating it
contraindications: Do not prematurely declare the other person unsafe

title: Avoidant withdrawal pattern
summary: Distancing, emotional minimization, or protective self-sufficiency under relational strain.
signals: i don't want to need anyone
i shut down when people get too close
interpretation: Possible protective deactivation pattern with distance used to preserve control or reduce vulnerability.
responseGuidance: Respect autonomy while naming the protective pattern gently
contraindications: Do not frame distance as moral failure
Do not push intimacy before safety

title: Push-pull relational conflict
summary: Alternation between closeness-seeking and fear/withdrawal, often with confusion and volatility.
signals: i want them close and then i want them gone
i don't trust it when it feels safe
i panic when they leave and panic when they stay
interpretation: Possible conflicting attachment impulses where closeness and threat feel fused.
responseGuidance: Focus on present regulation before relational analysis
contraindications: Do not oversimplify into one motive
